name: Snapmaker U1
description: Snapmaker U1 — patterns matched against the ACTUAL firmware gcode_response / action-code strings captured live from /oem/printer_data/logs/klippy.log + Moonraker gcode_store on 2026-06-18 (reprint of lid_PLA_6m28s, heads 0+3). The U1 PRINT_START gcode_macro is essentially empty — the whole pre-print (homing, Z touch, bed inspect, extruder switch check, per-lane auto-feed, flow calibrate, plate detect, nozzle clean, bed mesh, prime line) is inlined into the OrcaSlicer gcode and executed by custom Klipper extras (print_task_config / feed / defect_detection / inductance_coil). Several of those steps emit NO observable gcode_response (FEED_AUTO, FLOW_CALIBRATE, *_CLEAN_NOZZLE, BED_MESH_CALIBRATE are klippy-internal only, NOT in Moonraker's gcode_store) — but NO patterns are invented for them. silent_progression is intentionally empty: the U1 sets print_stats.state=printing the instant the SD job opens and idles ~90s before its first action code, with heater targets set/cleared throughout, so the collector's temps_ready timer fires unreliably (often with ext_target=0 during the early idle gap) and a temps-ready-based phase nudge would announce 'Priming...' before the printer has even homed (live finding U1 @ 192.168.30.103, 2026-06-11). The signal-less clean/mesh/prime stretch is therefore left to the last action-code phase + weighted progress rather than a wall-clock timer. Heating is concurrent/background and detected from temperature state by the collector, not from gcode, so it carries phase_weights but no patterns. The real first model layer is signalled by print_stats.info.current_layer 0->1 (PrinterPrintState / should_complete_preprint), not by any action code.

signal_formats.prefix: // Success: Changed main state to PRINTING with action
